official.name: Brian P. Kemp
official.office_address: Office of the Governor, Atlanta, Georgia

summary.narrative: Kemp campaigned on putting Georgians first, prioritizing economic growth, election security reform, public safety, education, and tax relief. Second term accomplishments include significant job creation (18,300+ jobs, $12.5B investment), tax cuts/rebates, anti-abortion legislation, gun rights expansion, and election law overhauls. Faced controversy over 2020 election decisions and voting law characterizations.
summary.grade_rationale: Strong economic delivery with measurable job/investment outcomes; tax relief implemented; core conservative priorities advanced. Deductions for election law controversy and limited detail on education/rural investment specifics.

promises.normalized: Deliver economic growth and job creation, especially in rural Georgia
Implement tax cuts and provide tax relief to Georgia families
Reform election rules and strengthen election security
Strengthen public safety and improve K-12 education quality

data_gaps: Specific education improvement metrics and legislation passed
Public safety outcomes and crime reduction data
Detailed breakdown of rural investment allocation
Tax cut implementation timeline and household benefit amounts
Election law litigation outcomes and legal challenges
Executive order details and effectiveness measures
Comparison of promises vs. 2018 first-term delivery
